originalPrompt: Create a full, minimal, production-ready landing page for a lab technician. Requirements: single HTML page served by Express; responsive layout; hero with title/subtitle; services list; contact form with client-side validation (no backend, just alert); palette strictly red/black/white; README with run/build/test steps; npm scripts: start, dev, test; tests using Jest to verify hero title and /health endpoint returns ok; no frontend frameworks; use plain CSS and JS.

subtasks.title: Initialize project and install dependencies
subtasks.description: Initialize npm and create the basic structure. Steps: run npm init -y; add directories public/ and tests/; add placeholder files server.js, public/index.html, public/styles.css, public/app.js; create .gitignore for node_modules and logs; install express as a dependency; install jest, supertest, jsdom, and nodemon as devDependencies.
subtasks.successCriteria: package.json exists with express as dependency and jest, supertest, jsdom, nodemon as devDependencies; public/ and tests/ directories present with placeholder files; npm install completes without errors; .gitignore excludes node_modules.

subtasks.title: Implement Express server with static hosting and health endpoint
subtasks.description: Create server.js to serve the single-page app. Steps: configure Express to serve static files from public/; implement GET /health returning JSON { status: 'ok' }; export the Express app for tests and start the server only when run directly; read PORT from env with fallback to 3000; add basic error logging.
subtasks.successCriteria: Running `node server.js` starts the server on PORT or 3000; GET /health responds 200 with JSON {"status":"ok"}; navigating to / serves index.html from public/.

subtasks.title: Build responsive single-page HTML skeleton
subtasks.description: Create semantic index.html. Steps: add meta charset and viewport for responsiveness; add hero section with clear title and subtitle; add services list (semantic list or cards) describing lab technician offerings; add contact form with name, email, and message fields plus submit button; link styles.css and app.js; provide IDs/classes for testing (e.g., #hero-title) and scripting.
subtasks.successCriteria: index.html loads with visible hero title and subtitle; services list and contact form are present with required fields and labels; document links to styles.css and app.js correctly; elements include stable selectors (e.g., id="hero-title").

subtasks.title: Implement red/black/white responsive CSS
subtasks.description: Author styles in public/styles.css. Steps: define CSS variables for red, black, white; ensure all colors in the site use only these variables; implement mobile-first layout with responsive breakpoints; style hero, services section, and contact form with accessible contrast and focus states; verify no other colors, shadows, or gradients introduce non-palette hues.
subtasks.successCriteria: Visual inspection confirms only red, black, and white are used; layout adapts from small to large screens without overflow; text is legible with sufficient contrast; no external frameworks are referenced.

subtasks.title: Add client-side form validation and submission alert
subtasks.description: Implement validation in public/app.js. Steps: attach submit handler to the contact form; validate required fields and email format with regex; display inline, accessible error messages and prevent default on invalid input; on valid submission, show an alert confirming receipt and reset the form; do not perform any network requests.
subtasks.successCriteria: Submitting with invalid input prevents submission and shows clear error messages near fields; submitting valid input triggers a single alert and resets the form; no fetch/XHR calls are made.

subtasks.title: Configure Jest and write tests for hero and health endpoint
subtasks.description: Set up Jest and author tests. Steps: configure Jest in package.json to use jsdom environment for DOM tests; write tests/ui.test.js to load public/index.html and assert presence and non-empty text of the hero title element (e.g., #hero-title); write tests/server.test.js using supertest to verify GET /health returns 200 and { status: 'ok' }; ensure server.js exports app without listening when required.
subtasks.successCriteria: `npm test` runs Jest and both tests pass locally; ui.test.js successfully reads index.html and finds the hero title; server.test.js successfully imports the app and asserts the /health response.

subtasks.title: Define npm scripts for start, dev, and test
subtasks.description: Add package.json scripts. Steps: set start to `node server.js`; set dev to `nodemon server.js`; set test to `jest`; optionally add a jest watch config if desired; verify each script works as intended on the local machine.
subtasks.successCriteria: `npm start` serves the app; `npm run dev` reloads on file changes; `npm test` executes Jest and returns exit code 0 when tests pass.

subtasks.title: Write README with run, build, and test steps
subtasks.description: Create README.md. Steps: include project overview and feature list; list prerequisites (Node.js version); provide installation instructions; document how to run in production (npm start) and in development (npm run dev); clarify build steps are not required for this project; document how to run tests and expected outcomes; note the red/black/white palette and no-frontend-framework constraint.
subtasks.successCriteria: README.md clearly explains install, run, build (not applicable), and test steps; a new developer can follow the instructions to run and test the app successfully.

decompositionStrategy: Iterative, test-oriented build: bootstrap project and dependencies, implement minimal Express server, create semantic HTML, layer in strict-palette CSS and client-side JS validation, then configure Jest and author endpoint/DOM tests. Wire npm scripts, and finalize with a clear README. Each step depends only on the prerequisites to avoid circular references.
